summary:
\boxed{C} correctly defines brachytherapy as placing radioactive materials inside or adjacent to tumors for localized treatment. It distinguishes this approach by its physical delivery method—direct implantation versus distant irradiation—as opposed to teletherapy techniques used externally.

The summary above contains no extraneous information beyond what was provided in Agent1’s original response; it strictly reflects only their stated rationale based on anatomical positioning and mechanism of action related to radioisotope deployment during cancer therapy.
